displayTitle: The Clone Wars: The Colossus of Destiny
synopsis:
During a battle over the planet Simocadia, Republic forces are ordered to withdraw, but Mace Windu ignores Yoda's warning that his personal feelings for the world may be clouding his judgment and drops to the surface in a missile-shaped pod, punching through a Separatist flagship on the way down. On the surface, Empress Sephani and Captain Tobin prepare to surrender, while her son Prince Yojan and his aide Demo recover an ancient artifact from an archeological site.

Fighting through waves of battle droids alongside Commander Ponds and his clone troopers, Windu recalls his long history with the planet, where he once crash-landed and single-handedly fought off invaders. He reaches the palace in time to save the Empress, who reveals that Yojan has joined the Separatists to revive a superweapon and forbids Mace from stopping her son, fearing it will only feed an endless cycle of war. Windu presses on anyway. Demo, claiming to oppose Yojan's plan, explains that Simocadia's ancient artisans destroyed themselves using colossal stone giants called the Ardana Shadex, then leads Mace into an ambush that Windu escapes by taking Demo hostage.

At the dig site, the torso of an Ardana Shadex has been unearthed, and Yojan prepares to activate it. Mace duels the prince and hurls his lightsaber into the activation port, but this only sends the colossus berserk. As it rampages toward the city, Yojan reveals it can only be stopped from within; he climbs inside and destroys it, and is crushed in the process. Mace leaves Simocadia a public enemy rather than the hero he once was, confessing to Yoda that the victory feels hollow, since the Empress's fear that the Republic will now dominate her world seems to be coming true.
title: The Clone Wars: The Colossus of Destiny
